content.body:
## Overview
Complete the foundational project setup including CI/CD pipeline and Microsoft Entra ID authentication.

## User Stories
- **US-AUTH-01**: As a LogNEXT employee, I want to sign in with my corporate Microsoft account so that I can securely access all company services.
- **US-AUTH-02**: As a LogNEXT employee, I want the app to remember my session so I don't have to sign in every time.

## Tasks
- [ ] #14 - Set up GitHub Actions CI/CD pipeline
- [ ] #15 - Implement silent token refresh
- [ ] #16 - Configure Graph API permissions
- [ ] #29 - Add biometric authentication support

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] GitHub Actions CI/CD pipeline builds and tests on PR
- [ ] MSAL integration with silent token refresh working
- [ ] Graph API permissions configured (Sites.Read.All, GroupMember.Read.All, People.Read)
- [ ] Entra ID app registration completed
- [ ] Session persists until explicit sign-out or token expiration
- [ ] Biometric/passwordless sign-in supported via Microsoft Authenticator

## Technical Notes
- Entra ID tenant already has app registration (Client ID: 8cebd652-bfab-4e10-ae5a-c67214ac9d03)
- Currently only User.Read scope is active - need additional permissions from IT admin
- Tokens stored in iOS Keychain via MSAL
- **Beads ID:** STAFF-kxd

## Status
🟡 Partially implemented - basic MSAL sign-in/sign-out exists, needs completion
content.title: [EPIC] Project Setup & Authentication

content.body:
## Overview
Design and implement the main home screen layout replicating the SharePoint SIU structure with native iOS navigation.

## Tasks
- [ ] #17 - Create HomeViewModel with section data
- [ ] #18 - Implement scrollable home layout
- [ ] #19 - Build section tile components
- [ ] Implement NavigationStack routing (Beads: STAFF-ujs.4)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Tab bar or scrollable home layout matching SIU design
- [ ] Section-based layout (Quick Access, Applications, Squads, Services, Resources)
- [ ] Navigation stack with proper routing between screens
- [ ] Pull-to-refresh functionality
- [ ] Responsive layout for different iPhone sizes

## Technical Notes
- Follow MVVM architecture with HomeViewModel
- Use SwiftUI NavigationStack for iOS 16+
- Section data driven by remote configuration
- **Beads ID:** STAFF-ujs

## Dependencies
- Blocked by: #11 (Remote Configuration System)

## Status
🔴 Not started - ContentView is placeholder only

milestone.title: MVP v1.0

content.body:
## Overview
Create a reusable WebView component for rendering Microsoft Forms and intranet pages with proper SSO integration.

## Tasks
- [ ] #20 - Create WKWebView UIViewRepresentable wrapper
- [ ] #21 - Implement SSO session sharing for WebView
- [ ] Add loading states and error handling (Beads: STAFF-nb8.3)
- [ ] Detect form submission completion (Beads: STAFF-nb8.4)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Reusable WKWebView wrapper using UIViewRepresentable
- [ ] SSO session sharing via ASWebAuthenticationSession
- [ ] Loading states with progress indicator
- [ ] Error handling with retry option
- [ ] Form submission completion detection (URL change observation)
- [ ] Pre-population of user data via URL parameters

## Technical Notes
- Use ASWebAuthenticationSession for SSO (Option A from HLD)
- WKWebView sandboxed - cookies not shared with Safari
- Clear WebView data on sign-out
- May need Option B (cookie injection) if SSO proves unreliable
- **Beads ID:** STAFF-nb8

## Dependencies
- Blocked by: #1 (Authentication)

## Status
🔴 Not started
content.title: [EPIC] WebView Component

content.body:
## Overview
Implement the four "submit" actions that open Microsoft Forms for employees to submit incidents, requests, material requests, and change management requests.

## User Stories
- **US-QA-01**: Submit IT incident from phone
- **US-QA-02**: Submit general IT request
- **US-QA-03**: Request IT equipment (laptop, monitor, etc.)
- **US-QA-04**: Submit change management request

## Tasks
- [ ] #27 - Implement submit request form launchers (all 4 forms)

Individual form tasks (Beads):
- [ ] Enviar Incidencia form (STAFF-lxv.1)
- [ ] Enviar Petición form (STAFF-lxv.2)
- [ ] Enviar Solicitud Material IT form (STAFF-lxv.3)
- [ ] Enviar Gestión de Cambio form (STAFF-lxv.4)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] "Enviar Incidencia" opens corresponding MS Form
- [ ] "Enviar Petición" opens corresponding MS Form
- [ ] "Enviar Solicitud de Material IT" opens corresponding MS Form
- [ ] "Enviar Gestión de Cambio" opens corresponding MS Form
- [ ] Forms pre-populated with user name/email from MS profile
- [ ] Submission confirmation displayed inline
- [ ] User can return to home screen without losing context

## Technical Notes
- Uses WebView component
- Form URLs from remote configuration
- Authentication inherited from app session
- **Beads ID:** STAFF-lxv

## Dependencies
- Blocked by: #3 (WebView Component)
- Blocked by: #11 (Remote Configuration - for URLs)

## Status
🔴 Not started
content.title: [EPIC] Submit Request Forms (Quick Access)

milestone.description: LogNEXT Staff Hub MVP - iOS Application. Replaces SharePoint SIU with native mobile experience. Includes auth, request forms, status views, app launcher, squads, and corporate resources.
milestone.title: MVP v1.0

content.body:
## Overview
Implement native SwiftUI list views for employees to track their submitted incidents, requests, material requests, and change management requests. Data fetched via Microsoft Graph API from SharePoint Lists.

## User Stories
- **US-QA-05**: View status of submitted incidents
- **US-QA-06**: View status of submitted requests
- **US-QA-07**: Track IT material requests
- **US-QA-08**: Track change management requests

## Tasks
- [ ] #22 - Create GraphService for SharePoint Lists
- [ ] #23 - Define data models for tickets
- [ ] #24 - Build native incident status list view
- [ ] Build native request status list view (Beads: STAFF-97k.4)
- [ ] Build native material request list view (Beads: STAFF-97k.5)
- [ ] Build native change management list view (Beads: STAFF-97k.6)
- [ ] Implement status filtering (Beads: STAFF-97k.7)
- [ ] Create ticket detail view (Beads: STAFF-97k.8)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Native list view for incidents with Subject, Status (color-coded), Department, Date, Urgency, Impact
- [ ] Native list view for requests with type, status, assigned to, resolution date
- [ ] Native list view for material requests with item, status, expected delivery
- [ ] Native list view for change management with description, approval stage, status, comments
- [ ] Detail view for each item type with full information
- [ ] Pull-to-refresh functionality
- [ ] Filtering by status (Open, In Progress, Closed)
- [ ] Color-coded status badges

## Technical Notes
- Graph API: GET /sites/{siteId}/lists/{listId}/items with $filter on CreatedBy
- SharePoint List GUIDs needed from IT admin (see HLD §4.1.2)
- Requires Sites.Read.All permission
- **Beads ID:** STAFF-97k

## Dependencies
- Blocked by: #1 (Authentication - Graph API permissions)
- Blocked by: #11 (Remote Configuration - for SharePoint site/list IDs)

## Status
🔴 Not started - GraphService skeleton exists but incomplete
content.title: [EPIC] Status Views - Graph API Integration

milestone.description: LogNEXT Staff Hub MVP - iOS Application. Replaces SharePoint SIU with native mobile experience. Includes auth, request forms, status views, app launcher, squads, and corporate resources.

content.body:
## Overview
Implement quick access to third-party business applications and Microsoft Office suite with deep link detection and SSO where supported.

## User Stories
- **US-APP-01**: Launch business apps (BoondManager, Cezanne, A3Innuva, LinkedIn) from Staff Hub
- **US-APP-02**: Quick links to Microsoft Office tools (Outlook, Teams, OneDrive, Calendar)

## Tasks
- [ ] #28 - Implement DeepLinkService and app launchers

Individual tasks (Beads):
- [ ] Configure LSApplicationQueriesSchemes (STAFF-7jb.1)
- [ ] Implement DeepLinkService (STAFF-7jb.2)
- [ ] Create Microsoft Office app launchers (STAFF-7jb.3)
- [ ] Implement third-party app launchers (STAFF-7jb.4)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Application tiles with name and icon
- [ ] Deep link detection via URL schemes (canOpenURL)
- [ ] Microsoft Office app launchers (ms-outlook://, msteams://, etc.)
- [ ] BoondManager launcher with SSO
- [ ] Cezanne OnDemand launcher with SSO
- [ ] A3Innuva web fallback (no SSO support)
- [ ] LinkedIn app/web launcher
- [ ] Fallback to web version in SFSafariViewController if native app not installed
- [ ] Redirect to App Store if Microsoft app not installed
- [ ] App list configurable via remote config

## Technical Notes
- LSApplicationQueriesSchemes must be configured in Info.plist
- BoondManager: SAML SSO confirmed
- Cezanne: SAML 2.0 confirmed
- A3Innuva: No SSO (separate auth required)
- URL schemes need discovery from vendors (see HLD §4.3.2)
- **Beads ID:** STAFF-7jb

## Dependencies
- Blocked by: #11 (Remote Configuration System)

## Status
🔴 Not started
content.title: [EPIC] Application Launcher

content.body:
## Overview
Display the list of LogNEXT Squads (teams) with member information fetched from Microsoft 365.

## User Stories
- **US-SQ-01**: See list of Squads to understand organizational structure
- **US-SQ-02**: See who belongs to each Squad to find the right contact

## Squads
Staff, Stark, Targaryen, Baratheon, Departamento IT, People, Big Daily DNN, ComercialLognext, Centro IA, Arryn

## Tasks
- [ ] Create Squads grid view (Beads: STAFF-arn.1)
- [ ] Fetch Squad members from Graph API (Beads: STAFF-arn.2)
- [ ] Build Squad detail view with contact cards (Beads: STAFF-arn.3)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Grid of Squad tiles with name and color-coded icon
- [ ] Squad detail page with members, description, and links
- [ ] Member list with names, roles, and profile photos (from M365)
- [ ] Contact card with email and Teams chat options
- [ ] Data fetched from remote config or Graph API

## Technical Notes
- Need to determine data source: M365 Groups, Teams teams, or manual list (see HLD §4.5)
- If M365 Groups: GET /groups/{groupId}/members
- Requires GroupMember.Read.All permission
- **Beads ID:** STAFF-arn

## Dependencies
- Blocked by: #1 (Authentication - Graph API permissions)
- Blocked by: #11 (Remote Configuration)
- IT to confirm Squads data source

## Status
🔴 Not started

content.body:
## Overview
Provide access to company directories, documentation, monitoring tools, and communications.

## User Stories
- **US-CR-01**: Search company directory for colleague contact info
- **US-CR-02**: See available distribution lists
- **US-CR-03**: Access user manuals and documentation
- **US-CR-04**: Access internal tracking dashboard
- **US-CR-05**: Access PRTG infrastructure monitoring (IT team)
- **US-CR-06**: Read company news and announcements
- **US-CR-07**: Quick access to public LogNEXT website
- **US-CR-08**: Access Basketball LogNEXT page

## Tasks
- [ ] Implement Contacts directory (Beads: STAFF-vk6.1)
- [ ] Implement resource launchers (Beads: STAFF-vk6.2)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] LogNEXT Contacts - native directory or WebView
- [ ] Distribution Lists - intranet page access
- [ ] User Manuals - documentation section access
- [ ] Internal Tracking Dashboard - authenticated WebView
- [ ] PRTG Monitor - web interface access
- [ ] News - news feed or intranet page
- [ ] LogNEXT Web - opens in system browser
- [ ] Basketball LogNEXT - intranet page or form
- [ ] All resource URLs configurable via remote config

## Technical Notes
- Contacts ideally native via Graph API (User.Read, People.Read)
- PRTG may require additional auth beyond MS SSO
- News could be native if backed by SharePoint list
- **Beads ID:** STAFF-vk6

## Dependencies
- Blocked by: #3 (WebView Component)
- Blocked by: #11 (Remote Configuration)
- Blocked by: #1 (Authentication - for Graph API contacts)

## Status
🔴 Not started

content.body:
## Overview
Implement a remote configuration system allowing IT to manage the app's service catalog without requiring app updates.

## User Story
- **US-CFG-01**: As IT admin, manage app service catalog remotely

## Tasks
- [ ] #25 - Define configuration JSON schema
- [ ] #26 - Implement ConfigService with caching
- [ ] Create bundled default configuration (Beads: STAFF-1lq.3)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] JSON configuration schema defined
- [ ] Config hosted on SharePoint (or lightweight API)
- [ ] App fetches config on launch
- [ ] Local caching for offline resilience
- [ ] Bundled default config as fallback
- [ ] Config includes: sections, items, URLs, icons, SharePoint IDs, app schemes

## Configuration Schema (from HLD)
\`\`\`json
{
  "version": 1,
  "sections": [...],
  "sharepoint": { "siteId": "...", "lists": {...} },
  "apps": { "boondmanager": {...}, ... }
}
\`\`\`

## Technical Notes
- Store in UserDefaults or file system
- Parse failures fall back to bundled config
- SwiftData optional for more complex caching
- **Beads ID:** STAFF-1lq

## Dependencies
- SharePoint file hosting confirmed by IT

## Status
🔴 Not started
content.title: [EPIC] Remote Configuration System

content.body:
## Overview
Implement comprehensive testing strategy including unit tests, integration tests, and UI tests.

## Tasks
- [ ] Write unit tests for service layer (Beads: STAFF-2c3.1)
- [ ] Write unit tests for model parsing (Beads: STAFF-2c3.2)
- [ ] Write UI tests for critical flows (Beads: STAFF-2c3.3)
- [ ] Implement accessibility features (Beads: STAFF-2c3.4)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Unit tests for service layer (GraphService, ConfigService, AuthService)
- [ ] Unit tests for model parsing
- [ ] Unit tests for URL scheme logic
- [ ] Integration tests for Graph API calls
- [ ] UI tests for critical flows (sign-in, submit form, view status, open app)
- [ ] Manual testing on physical devices (iPhone 14+)
- [ ] CI runs unit/UI tests on every PR
- [ ] Integration tests run on merge to main

## Testing Tools
- XCTest / Swift Testing
- XCUITest for UI tests
- GitHub Actions for CI

## Coverage Targets
- Service layer: 80%+
- Model parsing: 90%+
- Critical user flows: All covered

## Technical Notes
- **Beads ID:** STAFF-2c3

## Dependencies
- Blocked by: #1 (Authentication), #2 (Home Screen), #5 (Status Views)
- All feature epics substantially complete

## Status
🔴 Not started
content.title: [EPIC] Testing & Quality Assurance

milestone.description: LogNEXT Staff Hub MVP - iOS Application. Replaces SharePoint SIU with native mobile experience. Includes auth, request forms, status views, app launcher, squads, and corporate resources.
milestone.title: MVP v1.0

content.body:
## Overview
Prepare and submit the app to the Apple App Store.

## Tasks
- [ ] Enroll in Apple Developer Program (Beads: STAFF-fro.1)
- [ ] Create distribution certificate and profiles (Beads: STAFF-fro.2)
- [ ] Prepare App Store assets (Beads: STAFF-fro.3)
- [ ] Submit for App Store review (Beads: STAFF-fro.4)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Apple Developer Program enrollment (Organization) - $99/year
- [ ] D-U-N-S number for LogNEXT obtained (if needed)
- [ ] Distribution certificate and provisioning profile created
- [ ] App Store Connect record created for "LogNEXT Staff Hub"
- [ ] App screenshots for all required device sizes
- [ ] App Store description and keywords
- [ ] Privacy policy URL
- [ ] App review submission
- [ ] TestFlight beta testing before release

## Pre-Submission Checklist
- [ ] No private API usage
- [ ] App provides value beyond web links
- [ ] Accessibility compliance
- [ ] Performance acceptable on target devices

## Technical Notes
- Enrollment can take 1-2 weeks (D-U-N-S verification)
- Start in Week 1 to avoid blocking final submission
- App Store review typically 24-48 hours
- **Beads ID:** STAFF-fro

## Timeline Notes
- Enrollment can take 1-2 weeks (D-U-N-S verification)
- Start in Week 1 to avoid blocking final submission
- App Store review typically 24-48 hours

## Dependencies
- Blocked by: All features complete
- Blocked by: #12 (Testing)

## Status
🔴 Not started
content.title: [EPIC] App Store Preparation & Release

content.body:
**Parent:** [EPIC] Project Setup & Authentication (#1)
**Beads ID:** STAFF-kxd.4

## Description
Support Face ID/Touch ID via Microsoft Authenticator integration for passwordless sign-in.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Detect if Microsoft Authenticator is installed
- [ ] Enable biometric authentication when available
- [ ] Handle fallback gracefully to password auth
- [ ] Respect user's device biometric settings

## Technical Notes
- MSAL supports biometric via Microsoft Authenticator app
- Check `LAContext` for biometric availability
- May require additional Entra ID Conditional Access configuration
content.title: Add biometric authentication support

milestone.description: LogNEXT Staff Hub MVP - iOS Application. Replaces SharePoint SIU with native mobile experience. Includes auth, request forms, status views, app launcher, squads, and corporate resources.
milestone.title: MVP v1.0

content.body:
**Parent:** [EPIC] Project Setup & Authentication (#1)
**Beads ID:** STAFF-kxd.2

## Description
Add acquireTokenSilently flow to AuthManager for seamless session persistence.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Check Keychain for cached MSAL account on launch
- [ ] Attempt silent token renewal before showing login UI
- [ ] Navigate directly to home if silent auth succeeds
- [ ] Fall back to interactive login on failure

## Technical Notes
- MSAL handles refresh tokens in Keychain automatically
- Need to call acquireTokenSilently before acquireToken(interactive)
content.title: Implement silent token refresh

milestone.description: LogNEXT Staff Hub MVP - iOS Application. Replaces SharePoint SIU with native mobile experience. Includes auth, request forms, status views, app launcher, squads, and corporate resources.
milestone.title: MVP v1.0

content.body:
**Parent:** [EPIC] Project Setup & Authentication (#1)
**Beads ID:** STAFF-kxd.3

## Description
Update MSAL scopes and coordinate with IT admin for Entra ID consent.

## App Registration Details

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **Client ID** | `8cebd652-bfab-4e10-ae5a-c67214ac9d03` |
| **Tenant ID** | `b3c806dc-e5d8-44d0-b9e2-aedd316928ea` |
| **Redirect URI** | `msauth.com.lognext.app://auth` |

> Source: `Nexter/MSALAuth/AuthManager.swift`

## Required Permissions (Delegated)

| Permission | Purpose | Admin Consent Required |
|------------|---------|----------------------|
| `User.Read` | Sign in and read user profile | No ✅ (already active) |
| `Sites.Read.All` | Read SharePoint Lists (incidents, requests) | **Yes** |
| `GroupMember.Read.All` | Read squad/team membership | **Yes** |
| `People.Read` | Read user's relevant contacts | No |

## Steps for IT Admin (Entra Admin Center)

### 1. Access the App Registration

1. Go to [Microsoft Entra Admin Center](https://entra.microsoft.com)
2. Navigate to: **Identity** → **Applications** → **App registrations** → **All applications**
3. Search for Client ID: `8cebd652-bfab-4e10-ae5a-c67214ac9d03`

### 2. Add API Permissions

1. Click **API permissions** in the left sidebar
2. Click **+ Add a permission**
3. Select **Microsoft Graph**
4. Select **Delegated permissions**
5. Search and add each permission:
   - `Sites.Read.All`
   - `GroupMember.Read.All`
   - `People.Read`

### 3. Grant Admin Consent

1. Click **Grant admin consent for [Tenant Name]**
2. Confirm by clicking **Yes**

### 4. Verify

All permissions should show a green checkmark under "Status":

```
API / Permission name          Type        Status
─────────────────────────────────────────────────
Microsoft Graph
├── GroupMember.Read.All      Delegated   ✓ Granted
├── People.Read               Delegated   ✓ Granted
├── Sites.Read.All            Delegated   ✓ Granted
└── User.Read                 Delegated   ✓ Granted
```

## Feature Mapping

| App Feature | Permission Needed |
|-------------|-------------------|
| View incidents/requests from SharePoint Lists | `Sites.Read.All` |
| Show squad members and org chart | `GroupMember.Read.All` |
| Display IT team contacts | `People.Read` |

## Official Documentation

- [Grant tenant-wide admin consent](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity/enterprise-apps/grant-admin-consent)
- [Configure app API permissions](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity-platform/quickstart-configure-app-access-web-apis)
- [Microsoft Graph permissions overview](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/graph/permissions-overview)

---

## Action Items (Developer)

After IT admin grants consent:

- [ ] Update scopes array in `AuthManager.swift`:
  ```swift
  private let scopes = [
      "User.Read",
      "Sites.Read.All",
      "GroupMember.Read.All",
      "People.Read"
  ]
  ```
- [ ] Test token acquisition with new scopes
- [ ] Verify Graph API calls work with the new token
content.title: Configure Graph API permissions
